title: Subdomain Parsing and Tenant Identification at the Edge
description:
Implement hostname parsing at the Cloudflare edge to extract `tenant_slug` and `region` from the request URL, enabling automatic tenant routing.

## Hostname Format

- Dedicated tenant: `toyota.us1.datatoolspro.com` → `tenant_slug=toyota`, `region=us1`
- Pooled tenant: `us1.datatoolspro.com` or `app.us1.datatoolspro.com` → pooled mode for region `us1`

## Requirements

- Cloudflare Worker extracts tenant_slug and region from hostname
- Pooled hosts are treated as a reserved namespace (never as a tenant)
- Invalid hostnames return 404/tenant-not-found
acceptance_criteria: Worker correctly parses tenant_slug and region from dedicated tenant hostnames
Pooled hostnames are identified and routed to pooled mode
Reserved hostnames (app, www, api) are never treated as tenant slugs
Invalid or unrecognized hostnames return a 404 tenant-not-found response
implementation_notes: Cloudflare Worker-based hostname parsing. The Worker sits at the edge and determines routing before the request reaches the origin. Must coordinate with the reserved subdomains list (MBT-1278).
